hook: TLS trust depends on a chain of signed certificates, not a single file.
cta: Pin roots carefully and monitor intermediate certificate expiry.

storyboardBeats.objective: Introduce why certificate chains establish trust.
Server presents leaf certificate for its hostname.
Intermediate CA signs the leaf and links to a trusted root.
Client trusts only roots in its local trust store.
Chain validation checks signatures, expiry, and hostname policy.

narrationPlaceholders.scriptIntent: Introduce why certificate chains establish trust.
Server presents leaf certificate for its hostname.
Intermediate CA signs the leaf and links to a trusted root.
Client trusts only roots in its local trust store.
Chain validation checks signatures, expiry, and hostname policy.

transitionToNext.rationale: Broken trust chains enable person-in-the-middle attacks.
